query: Describe the tea fields of Minami-Yamashiro village in Kyoto. Just the village, not Uji.
reasoning: RULE A applies: wrong tool was called — get_japan_heritage returns 日本遺産 cultural narrative stories, not tea field tourism data. The response body is dominated by a browse_advisory nav-chrome block that correctly identifies 'tea field' intent and redirects to search_area/get_local_specialty, mentioning Minami-Yamashiro by name in the note. However, zero actual tea field data for Minami-Yamashiro is returned. Per RULE A, wrong-tool with some advisory utility scores 2 on intent (not 1), other dims drop by at most 1. Specificity hits 1 because no named assets at all were returned. travel_feasibility is scored 4 (not applicable — no itinerary was proposed, so no feasibility failure).
missing_data: Any substantive data on Minami-Yamashiro village tea fields: named tea gardens, growing areas, cultivar types (e.g. Yamashiro-cha), harvest seasons, scenic viewpoints, access from Kyoto city, or GI/specialty records distinguishing Minami-Yamashiro from Uji.
improvement_hint: The intent classifier should detect 'tea field' + specific municipality (Minami-Yamashiro) and route to get_local_specialty(prefecture='Kyoto') + search_area(q='南山城村 茶畑') instead of get_japan_heritage.
